successCriteria.description: Auto-heal is opt-in via .tiki/config.json with workflow.autoHeal.{enabled,maxAttempts,categories}; absent file disables auto-heal by default
When verification fails and auto-heal is enabled, execute.md instructs Claude to capture error output, categorize it, and attempt a targeted fix
Auto-heal attempts are capped at config.workflow.autoHeal.maxAttempts (default 3); on exhaustion, Claude falls back to manual <next-actions> recovery
Each heal attempt is logged as a HealAttempt record in .tiki/state.json under the phase's healAttempts array; the manual fallback summarizes all attempts
Errors are categorized into build-error, type-error, test-failure, lint-error, or other, with brief healing-strategy guidance per category
Source execute.md (packages/framework/commands/execute.md) and mirror (packages/framework/.claude/commands/tiki/execute.md) are byte-for-byte identical
pnpm build from repo root passes with no TypeScript errors

phases.title: Add <auto-heal> block to execute.md (source + mirror)
phases.content:
Modify `packages/framework/commands/execute.md` to add a new `<auto-heal>` XML block describing the opt-in heal loop. Place it AFTER the existing `<errors>` section and BEFORE `<next-actions>`.

The `<auto-heal>` block must cover:
1. **Opt-in check** — read `.tiki/config.json`, look for `workflow.autoHeal.enabled === true`. Treat missing file as disabled.
2. **Heal loop** — when verification fails AND auto-heal is enabled:
   a. Capture error output verbatim (preserve filenames, line numbers, diagnostic messages)
   b. Categorize the error: `build-error` / `type-error` / `test-failure` / `lint-error` / `other`
   c. Read prior attempts from `.tiki/state.json` at `activeWork["issue:{N}"].phase.healAttempts`
   d. Stop conditions: attempts >= maxAttempts (default 3), category not in allow-list, or repeating same error
   e. Attempt a targeted fix and re-run verification
   f. Append a `HealAttempt` record `{ attempt, timestamp, errorCategory, errorSummary, fixApplied, outcome }` to `phase.healAttempts` regardless of outcome
3. **Healing strategies** per category (brief guidance, not prescription):
   - build-error: smallest mechanical fix (imports, signatures, missing serde defaults)
   - type-error: re-read types; prefer ternary over intermediate booleans for discriminated-union narrowing
   - test-failure: decide whether test or implementation diverges from phase goal
   - lint-error: apply suggested fix
   - other: fall through to manual recovery
4. **Fallback** — when exhausted, present manual `<next-actions>` "After failure" flow with a summary of all heal attempts so the user knows what was tried.

Also update `<instructions>`: change the verification-failure step to reference `<auto-heal>`. Specifically replace `If verification fails, offer recovery options` with `If verification fails, follow <auto-heal> (when enabled in .tiki/config.json) before offering manual recovery options`.

After editing the source, copy it byte-for-byte to `packages/framework/.claude/commands/tiki/execute.md`. Verify with `Get-FileHash` that the hashes match.
verification: packages/framework/commands/execute.md contains an <auto-heal> block placed between <errors> and <next-actions>
<auto-heal> covers: opt-in check, 8-step heal loop, per-category healing strategies, manual fallback with attempts summary
packages/framework/.claude/commands/tiki/execute.md is byte-for-byte identical to the source (Get-FileHash matches)
pnpm build from repo root passes

phases.title: Create .tiki/config.json with default auto-heal schema
verification: workflow.autoHeal.categories includes build-error, type-error, test-failure, lint-error
File is valid JSON (parses without error)
